title: Onboard 2 further scripts/styles via the playbook
context: M4 validates that the 'add a script' playbook actually works for contributors beyond the core team. Two further scripts or styles — candidates include Latin continuous-cursive, Devanagari, or Hangul, chosen by reviewer availability — are onboarded by following the playbook exactly, demonstrating that new scripts can be added reproducibly without core-maintainer heroics. Each must go through the full medium-risk gate (script-literate reviewer, provenance gate, accessibility variants).
objective: Using the playbook-021 add-a-script process, onboard two further scripts or styles (specific scripts TBD by reviewer availability), each with schema-valid models, gate-verified provenance, script-literate reviewer sign-off, and accessibility variants.
acceptanceCriteria: Two further scripts or styles (e.g. Latin continuous-cursive and Devanagari, or Hangul and another — specific choices determined by which script-literate reviewer is secured first) onboarded following the playbook-021 process exactly.
Each script has: schema-valid GlyphModel JSON, gate-verified open font (gate-002 PASS artifact), SOURCES.md with snapshots, script-literate reviewer sign-off, and accessibility variants rendered.
Playbook is followed without deviating to core-maintainer shortcuts — any gap found in the playbook is documented as a follow-up task for playbook-021.
Per-script provenance-gate green; CC-BY-4.0 (or CC-BY-SA-4.0 where compelled) licensing with attribution on all outputs.
resources: PLAN.md section 9 M4 (2 further scripts via playbook), section 16 Q3/Q4 (cursive and script-sequencing open questions)
output: Two additional scripts/styles onboarded: data/<script>/ directories each with schema-valid models, SOURCES.md, reviewer sign-off, and generated trace-sheet PDFs — committed CC-BY-4.0.
